refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over agreed duration (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage hauled), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety alerts and policy constraints were honored during measurement period.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or teleoperated) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The telemetry system shall continuously measure and report task progress (volume, tonnage, cycle count) to enable productivity tracking.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety policies and operational constraints without blocking legitimate task execution.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-payload, low-cycle-time vehicle with real-time telemetry and operator feedback loop (local or remote) such that task throughput meets or exceeds productivity_target under representative conditions.

DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (payload, cycle time), operator interface quality (latency, situational awareness), and safety constraint enforcement; optimize each independently and validate integrated performance.

DP-C: Establish a closed-loop productivity control system: measure actual productivity in real-time, compare against target, and adjust task parameters (speed, payload, cycle strategy) dynamically to maintain or exceed target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (payload, cycle time), operator interface quality (latency, situational awareness), and safety constraint enforcement; optimize each independently and validate integrated performance.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time depends on operator command latency (teleoperation coupling).
Safety constraint enforcement may reduce achievable cycle time (safety-productivity trade-off).
Telemetry accuracy affects productivity measurement and feedback loop fidelity.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B explicitly acknowledges these couplings by decomposing into sub-DPs (vehicle, operator interface, safety services). Coupling is managed through clear sub-FR allocation and interface definition (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). This is preferable to DP-A (monolithic) or DP-C (dynamic control, which introduces real-time feedback complexity and risk).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity_target is achievable with available vehicle technology and operator skill level.
Assumption: Worksite safety policies do not impose constraints that reduce cycle time below target.
Risk: Teleoperation latency may degrade operator responsiveness and increase cycle time; mitigation via DP-B sub-FR on operator interface quality.
Risk: Safety constraint enforcement may conflict with productivity; mitigation via explicit safety-productivity trade-off analysis in sub-FR-001.4.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: None at this level. DP-B is well-structured for decomposition and supports clear allocation to sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Proceed to sub-FR decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to achieve productivity KPI. Owns the productivity measurement, reporting, and compliance verification logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports task progress (volume moved, tonnage, cycle count, timestamps) to Site Manager and Tele-operator for productivity tracking and real-time feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task definitions (dig location, load target, haul destination, payload limits) from Site Manager or Tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution propagates safety alerts and policy violations from Safety Services to Site Manager and Safety Manager for awareness and intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for teleoperation command/feedback and telemetry/alert transmission in remote operation mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. Productivity KPI is a system-level outcome that requires orchestration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Containment is high because the FR is explicitly about the NGHE solution's capability. Minimal diffusion: only measurement/reporting logic may be distributed to sub-modules.

Vehicle contributes to productivity via cycle time and payload capacity, but does not own the KPI. Productivity is a system-level outcome that depends on operator interface quality and safety constraints. High diffusion: Vehicle alone cannot achieve productivity target without Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation enables remote operation and situational awareness, but does not own the productivity KPI. Productivity depends on vehicle performance and safety constraints. High diffusion: Teleoperation is a capability enabler, not the primary owner of productivity outcome.

Safety Services enforces constraints that affect productivity, but does not own the KPI. Productivity is a business outcome; safety is a constraint. High diffusion: Safety Services is a constraint provider, not the primary owner of productivity achievement.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level KPI that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the productivity measurement, reporting, and compliance verification logic. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute to productivity but do not own the KPI.
containment_risks: Risk: Productivity measurement logic may be distributed across Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (operator latency), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Mitigation: Centralize productivity measurement and reporting in NGHE Solution; sub-modules report raw data (cycle time, latency, constraint violations) via Telemetry interface.

Risk: Productivity target may be unachievable due to vehicle limitations or safety constraints. Mitigation: Validate achievability during requirements phase; allocate sub-targets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Risk: Telemetry interface may not provide sufficient granularity for productivity tracking. Mitigation: Define Telemetry schema to include task progress, cycle time, operator latency, and safety constraint violations.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the Telemetry interface schema to include: (1) task progress (volume, tonnage, cycle count), (2) cycle time and operator latency, (3) safety constraint violations. This ensures NGHE Solution can accurately measure and report productivity without ambiguity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (decomposition into vehicle performance, operator interface, and safety constraint enforcement) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI). The DP is logically sound and supports clear allocation to sub-systems. No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: Productivity measurement logic is distributed across Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (operator latency), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Mitigation: Centralize measurement in NGHE Solution; sub-modules report raw data.
Productivity target achievement depends on all three sub-systems. If any sub-system underperforms, the KPI may not be met. Mitigation: Allocate sub-targets and validate integrated performance.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for the productivity KPI. It owns the measurement, reporting, and compliance verification logic. Sub-modules contribute to productivity but do not own the KPI. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and support clear data flow.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trials. Measure task completion (volume, tonnage, cycle count) and elapsed time. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate safety compliance. Requires instrumentation of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to report data via Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the productivity KPI and measurement logic. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services contribute to productivity via sub-FRs (cycle time, operator interface quality, safety constraint enforcement). Sub-FRs are well-defined and support clear ownership allocation.

recommended_revisions: Define Telemetry interface schema to include task progress, cycle time, operator latency, and safety constraint violations.
Allocate sub-targets to Vehicle (cycle time, payload), Teleoperation (operator latency, situational awareness), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement without blocking legitimate tasks).
Validate achievability of productivity_target during requirements phase; conduct trade-off analysis if target is unachievable.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (vehicle performance, operator interface, safety constraint enforcement) and sub-DPs. The refined FR and DP-B are sound and support clear allocation to sub-systems. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary. Proceed to decompose sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or teleoperated) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The telemetry system shall continuously measure and report task progress (volume, tonnage, cycle count) to enable productivity tracking.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety policies and operational constraints without blocking legitimate task execution.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with available vehicle technology and operator skill level.
Worksite safety policies do not impose constraints that reduce cycle time below target.
Telemetry interface provides sufficient granularity for productivity measurement (task progress, cycle time, operator latency, safety constraint violations).
NGHE Solution owns the productivity measurement, reporting, and compliance verification logic; sub-modules contribute via sub-FRs.
open_risks: Teleoperation latency may degrade operator responsiveness and increase cycle time; mitigation via DP-B sub-FR on operator interface quality.
Safety constraint enforcement may conflict with productivity; mitigation via explicit safety-productivity trade-off analysis in sub-FR-001.4.
Productivity target may be unachievable due to vehicle limitations or safety constraints; mitigation via achievability validation during requirements phase.
Productivity measurement logic may be distributed across sub-modules; mitigation via centralized measurement in NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Productivity_target value (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period duration.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies that constrain task execution.
Vehicle specifications (payload capacity, cycle time, available equipment).
Teleoperation system specifications (command latency, feedback bandwidth, situational awareness capabilities).
Safety Services specifications (constraint types, alert mechanisms, policy enforcement logic).
Telemetry interface schema (task progress, cycle time, operator latency, safety constraint violations).
